title: inula adenophylla
intro: Inula adenophylla, also known as the Golden Ragwort, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and hairy leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Inula adenophylla include relief from digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory problems like asthma and bronchitis. Inula adenophylla can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine to promote overall well-being and improve immune function.
